scenarios.title: Assessment freshness is evaluated before current-site claims are used
scenarios.given: An assessment input includes a scan date that is stale or missing
scenarios.when: the generator prepares a client website build brief
scenarios.then: the output flags the assessment freshness risk and requires verification before making current-site claims

scenarios.title: Missing client details become questions instead of invented claims
scenarios.given: client intake is missing reviews, licenses, service guarantees, service areas, staff names, or project proof
scenarios.when: the generator prepares the brief and Codex build prompt
scenarios.then: the output lists the missing details as client questions or content-needed items and does not invent the claims

scenarios.title: Generated client artifacts are client-safe
scenarios.given: the generator has access to central MDE context and project-specific client inputs
scenarios.when: it produces a launch pack for a future client repo
scenarios.then: the launch pack includes only client-safe local MDE seeds and excludes central private ledgers, cross-client lessons, and confidential internal strategy

scenarios.title: Required launch pack artifacts are generated together
scenarios.given: valid assessment evidence and required client intake are available
scenarios.then: it produces client questions, website mission, technical brief, content-needed checklist, validation strategy suggestion, deployment checklist, and codex-build.prompt.md

scenarios.title: Generated prompts enforce the Northvalley technical baseline
scenarios.given: a future client website build prompt is generated
scenarios.when: the prompt describes the implementation stack and validation checks
scenarios.then: it requires Northvalley-Intelligence repo target, main branch, Next.js, TypeScript, React, npm, package-lock.json, Node 22, OpenNext Cloudflare, wrangler, ESLint, typecheck, Vitest, Playwright, and npm audit

scenarios.title: Unsupported assessment failure claims require evidence or verification
scenarios.given: an assessment mentions broken links, broken images, missing service-area pages, missing Google Business Profile, or missing schema without exact evidence
scenarios.then: it preserves exact evidence when available and otherwise marks the recommendation as verify-before-implementing

scenarios.title: The generated handoff is directly usable in a new repo
scenarios.given: a generated launch pack is complete
scenarios.when: Ferosh starts a new client website repo
scenarios.then: the codex-build.prompt.md is standalone and the client questions and content-needed checklist clearly separate launch-blocking items from post-launch items

scenarios.title: Client can submit the intake without answering technical questions
scenarios.given: a client opens a tokenized intake link
scenarios.when: they complete the required business, offering, location, conversion, and consent fields
scenarios.then: they can submit the intake while the technical details section remains collapsed and unanswered

scenarios.title: Intake portal does not collect secrets
scenarios.given: a client opens the optional technical section
scenarios.when: they answer domain, DNS, Cloudflare, analytics, or integration questions
scenarios.then: the form asks only about status and ownership and never asks for passwords, API keys, registrar logins, Cloudflare tokens, or Google credentials

scenarios.title: Assessment defaults are suggestions that require confirmation
scenarios.given: assessment or demand data provides suggested services, locations, contact details, or current-site findings
scenarios.when: the intake form prepopulates those values
scenarios.then: the client can confirm, edit, or remove each suggestion and unconfirmed values are not treated as public launch-package claims

scenarios.title: Submitted intake maps to launch-package artifacts
scenarios.given: a submitted intake has the minimal required fields and optional asset/proof details
scenarios.when: Ferosh marks it ready for launch-package generation
scenarios.then: the generator can create the client summary, missing-info checklist, content-needed checklist, website brief, validation plan, deployment notes, and codex-build.prompt.md from structured fields

scenarios.title: Generated client website prompts prevent public AI and process wording
scenarios.given: a generated Codex build prompt will create or edit public client website routes
scenarios.when: the generator prepares the launch pack
scenarios.then: the prompt requires every public route to be reviewed and automatically scanned for internal/process phrases, with remaining AI/process wording reported as 0 before handoff

scenarios.title: Generated client websites do not publish placeholders or invented proof
scenarios.given: client intake does not include testimonials, reviews, project stories, awards, credentials, service claims, locations, or approved photos
scenarios.when: the generator prepares public website requirements
scenarios.then: the prompt requires those sections to stay off public routes until supplied and approved, and lists client-dependent items only in private handoff/content-needed artifacts

scenarios.title: Generated handoff aligns GitHub and production evidence
scenarios.given: a generated client website build is deployed to production
scenarios.when: the build agent prepares the handoff
scenarios.then: the prompt requires local validation, production validation, committed changes, pushed origin/main, deployed URL/version, and aligned local main, origin/main, and production evidence
